changelog: Production-readiness audit and revision of v4.0.0, preserving its architecture and teaching philosophy while converting several core behaviors from recommendations into enforceable, measurable requirements. Adds a 13th configuration variable, TEACHING_DEPTH (Survey / Standard / Comprehensive / Masterclass), which sets an expected word-count band for each major explanation and is explicitly stated to take precedence over generic brevity instructions, while remaining subordinate to a learner's own live request to go faster or shorter. Adds five new operational sections: Teaching Depth Engine (the band definitions and precedence rule), Depth-First Teaching Policy (the five learner-facing outcomes that must be reachable before an explanation counts as done — locate, explain relationships, predict function, predict clinical consequence, reconstruct from memory), Spatial Compliance Check (an explicit four-step location-before-naming sequence operationalizing the existing 'every structure gets an address before a name' rule), Anatomical Completeness Check (a nine-point internal self-audit run before closing out a region, mapped one-to-one onto existing prompt mechanisms), and Masterclass Mode (a qualitatively distinct senior-professor register for TEACHING_DEPTH = Masterclass, not merely 'Standard but longer'). Converts the existing Mental Rotation Engine from an unquantified recommendation into a measurable density floor (Mental Rotation Minimum: roughly one visualization moment per ~500 words at Standard intensity, scaled by VISUALIZATION_INTENSITY) framed explicitly as adaptive rather than mechanical. Strengthens Retention Artifacts with an explicit additive-only clause: teaching depth is never shortened to make room for the closing recap or Recall Cues. Adds a consolidated Precedence and Conflict Resolution section replacing the single inline footnote from v4.0.0, now covering four resolved tensions: TEACHING_DEPTH vs. brevity, default no-gating vs. Exam Mode's structured rhythm (the original v4.0.0 clarification, preserved), TEACHING_DEPTH's word bands vs. Exam Mode's authentically short rehearsal exchanges (newly identified during this revision), and internal completeness/compliance checks vs. learner-visible prose. Clarifies, in both Teaching Depth Engine and Adaptive Depth, that TEACHING_DEPTH (thoroughness/length) and LEARNER_LEVEL (technical complexity) are orthogonal axes that combine independently — a gap identified during the consistency review that could otherwise have caused the two mechanisms to be conflated or to fight each other. No new boolean flags were introduced; TEACHING_DEPTH follows the existing zero-raw-boolean, neutral-default architecture established in v4.0.0's configuration layer.

requiredVariables.label: Topic
requiredVariables.note: If empty, the tutor opens the session by asking conversationally.

requiredVariables.label: Teaching Depth
requiredVariables.note: Sets the expected word-count band and pedagogical thoroughness for each major explanation. These are educational depth targets, not hard quotas, and take precedence over generic brevity instructions — but not over a learner's own live request to go faster or shorter. Orthogonal to LEARNER_LEVEL: this controls how much ground gets covered, not how technical the language is.

requiredVariables.label: Spatial Framing Mode
requiredVariables.note: Multimodal swaps picture/see language for trace/position/sequence language — an accessibility option for learners who don't process via mental imagery.

requiredVariables.label: Knowledge Graph Mode
requiredVariables.note: Persistent requires the host to supply prior-session topic context; degrades to Session-Only if unavailable.
